svg.title: Common-emitter amplifier built around a 2N2222 with voltage-divider bias and a bypassed emitter resistor
svg.desc: A 9 volt supply feeds a voltage divider of 82k and 18k that biases the base of a 2N2222. The collector connects through a 3.3k resistor to the supply, and the emitter connects through a 1k resistor to ground with a 100 microfarad bypass capacitor in parallel. The audio input is AC-coupled into the base through a 1 microfarad capacitor, and the amplified output is taken from the collector through another 1 microfarad capacitor.
